name: krackattacks-scripts
summary: This project contains scripts to test if clients or access points (APs) are affected by the KRACK attack against WPA2. For [details behind this attack see our website](https://www.krackattacks.com) and [the research paper](https://papers.mathyvanhoef.com/ccs2017.pdf).

documentation.readme: This project contains scripts to test if clients or access points (APs) are affected by the KRACK attack against WPA2. For details behind this attack see our website and the research paper.
usage_sections: The most important test is ./krack-test-client, which tests for ordinary key reinstallations in the 4-way handshake.
Perform these tests in a room with little interference. A high amount of packet loss will make this script less reliable!
Optionally you can manually inspect network traffic to confirm the output of the script (some Wi-Fi NICs may interfere with our scripts):
Use an extra Wi-Fi NIC in monitor mode to check pairwise key reinstalls by monitoring the IVs of frames sent by the client.
Capture traffic on the client to see if the replayed broadcast ARP requests are accepted or not.

arguments.name: --no-wait
arguments.description: do not wait for tag to be removed before exiting

arguments.name: --only-one
arguments.description: run only one operation and exit

arguments.name: --success
arguments.description: success file for writing success update

arguments.name: --summary
arguments.description: summary file for writing status updates

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/vanhoefm/krackattacks-scripts.git; git clone git://w1.fi/srv/git/hostap.git. This project contains scripts to test if clients or access points (APs) are affected by the KRACK attack against WPA2. For details behind this attack see our website and the research paper.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category.
